---
title: "2.4 Nested if Statements"
subtitle: "Part 1: Multiway Selection and Trace Questions"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Nested if statements

If statements can be nested inside other if statements.

<span class="term">Nested if statements</span> consist of if, if-else, or if-else-if statements within if, if-else, or if-else-if statements.

The Boolean expression of the inner nested if statement is evaluated only if the Boolean expression of the outer if statement evaluates to <span class="mark">true</span>.

```java
if (boolean expression)
{   // This nested if is executed if outer if is true
    if (boolean expression)
    {
        statement;
    }
}
```

## Multiway Selection

### `else if`

A single if/else statement allows us to select between 2 branches of code.

With nested if/else statements, we can pick between 3 or more branches of code.

A <span class="term">multi-way selection</span> (if-else-if) is used when there are a series of expressions with different segments of code for each condition.

## Multiway Execution

### First true expression wins

Multi-way selection is performed such that <span class="mark">no more than one segment</span> of code is executed based on the first expression that evaluates to true.

If no expression evaluates to true and there is a trailing else statement, then the body of the else is executed.

## Else If Pattern

### Three-way choice

Just add `else if` for each possibility after the first `if`, and `else` before the last possibility.

```java
// 3 way choice with else if
if (boolean expression)
{
   statement1;
}
else if (boolean expression)
{
   statement2;
}
else
{
   statement3;
}
```

## Code Task

### `activecode:: TryElseIf`

Textbook prompt: Run the code below and try changing the value of `x` to get each of the three possible lines in the conditional to print.

## Code Window

### `activecode:: TryElseIf`

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
public class TryElseIf
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        int x = 2;
        if (x < 0)
        {
            System.out.println("x is negative");
        }
        else if (x == 0)
        {
            System.out.println("x is 0");
        }
        else
        {
            System.out.println("x is positive");
        }
        System.out.println("after conditional");
    }
}
```
:::

## Test Requirements

### `activecode:: TryElseIf`

Runestone checks that:

::: {.tight-list}
- `x = 2;` has been changed
- the new `x` assignment is zero or negative
- the first output line matches `x is negative` when `x` is negative
- the first output line matches `x is zero` when `x` is zero
:::

## Flowchart

### Conditional with 3 options

Here is a flowchart for a conditional with 3 options like in the code above.

![The order that statements execute in a conditional with 3 options: if, else if, and else](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-4-nested-if-statements/Condition-three.png){fig-align="center" width="44%"}

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: trace-nested-if`

What does the following code print when `x` has been set to `-5`?

```java
if (x < 0)
{
   System.out.println("x is negative");
}
else if (x == 0)
{
   System.out.println("x is zero");
}
else
{
   System.out.println("x is positive");
}
```

Options: `x is negative`, `x is zero`, `x is positive`.

## Answer Reasoning

### `mchoice:: trace-nested-if`

Correct answer: `x is negative`.

When `x` is equal to `-5`, the condition `x < 0` is true. The later else-if and else branches do not execute.

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: trace-nested-ifs2`

What does the following code print when `x` has been set to `2000`?

```java
if (x < 0)
{
   System.out.println("x is negative");
}
else if (x == 0)
{
   System.out.println("x is zero");
}
else
{
   System.out.println("x is positive");
}
```

Options: `x is negative`, `x is zero`, `x is positive`.

## Answer Reasoning

### `mchoice:: trace-nested-ifs2`

Correct answer: `x is positive`.

The first condition is false and `x` is not equal to zero, so the else branch executes.

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: trace-nested-ifs3`

What does the following code print when `x` has been set to `.8`?

::: {.code-scroll .compact}
```java
if (x < .25)
{
    System.out.println("first quartile");
}
else if (x < .5)
{
    System.out.println("second quartile");
}
else if (x < .75)
{
    System.out.println("third quartile");
}
else
{
    System.out.println("fourth quartile");
}
```
:::

## Answer Reasoning

### `mchoice:: trace-nested-ifs3`

Correct answer: `fourth quartile`.

The first three conditions are false for `.8`; the else branch prints whenever `x` is greater than or equal to `.75`.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- explain when the inner nested if is evaluated
- describe multiway selection as first-true-branch execution
- use `else if` branches for 3 or more choices
- trace why later branches are skipped after a true condition
- identify the final else as the branch for no true condition
:::

## End

### Continue to Part 2

[Next: Debugging and dangling else →](2-4-part-2-debugging-and-dangling-else.html)
